## Problem Statement
<!-- What user need or system gap does this address? Frame in terms of outcomes, not solutions. -->
<!-- Example: "As a user, I want to track my reading progress per book so I can see how far along I am." -->


## Proposed Solution
<!-- Describe your recommended approach at a high level. -->


## Architectural Considerations

### Components & Layers Affected
<!-- Which parts of the middleware are touched? Check all that apply. -->
- [ ] Controller / REST endpoints
- [ ] Service layer
- [ ] Repository / Data access
- [ ] Domain model / Entities
- [ ] DTOs / API contracts
- [ ] Security / Auth
- [ ] Configuration / Properties
- [ ] Middleware filters or interceptors

### Data Model Changes
<!-- Describe any new tables, columns, or entity relationships. Include a brief schema sketch if helpful. -->
<!-- Example: "Add a `reading_progress` table with columns: id, book_id (FK), current_page, total_pages, last_read_at." -->


### API Contract Changes
<!-- List any new or modified endpoints. Specify HTTP method, path, request/response shape. -->
<!-- Example: "PUT /api/books/{id}/progress — Request: { currentPage: int } — Response: 200 with updated BookProgressDTO" -->


### Dependencies Introduced
<!-- Any new libraries, external services, or internal module dependencies? -->


### Scalability & Performance
<!-- Will this affect query performance, memory, throughput? Any indexing or caching considerations? -->


### Security Considerations
<!-- Auth requirements, data visibility, input validation, rate limiting, etc. -->
